title: On descending cohomology geometrically
speaker: Sebastian Casalaina-Martin
abstract: In this talk I will present some joint work with Jeff Achter concerning the problem of determining when the cohomology of a smooth projective variety over the rational numbers can be modeled by an abelian variety. The primary motivation is a problem posed by Barry Mazur. We provide an answer to Mazur's question in two situations. First, we show that the third cohomology group can be modeled by the cohomology of an abelian variety over the rationals provided the Chow group of points is supported on a curve.

proof_stages.label: Mazur's phantom abelian variety problem
proof_stages.summary: The talk formulates Mazur's question asking when H^3 of a smooth projective variety over Q with H^{3,0}=0 is realized, up to twist, as H^1 of an abelian variety over Q, and motivates it from Frobenius divisibility, Tate-Honda, and intermediate Jacobians.

proof_stages.label: Prym construction prototype for geometric descent
proof_stages.summary: Mumford's construction of the intermediate Jacobian of a cubic threefold via projection from a line, a discriminant double cover, and a Prym variety is presented as the geometric model that Beauville generalizes to quadric fibrations and that can be descended over Q.

proof_stages.label: Chow-zero support theorem
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem is stated: if CH_0 of X_C is supported on a curve, then an abelian variety J over Q exists with H^1(J_{ ar Q},Q_l) isomorphic to H^3(X_{ ar Q},Q_l)(1), with consequences and limitations explained through Mumford-type vanishing and rationally connected geometry.

proof_stages.label: Reduction through Murre algebraic representatives
proof_stages.summary: After recalling regular morphisms from algebraically trivial Chow groups and Murre's algebraic representative Ab^2(X), the proof is reduced to descending Ab^2(X_{ ar Q}) to Q and proving its H^1 has the same dimension as H^3(X).

proof_stages.label: Galois descent of algebraic representatives
proof_stages.summary: For a smooth projective variety over a perfect field, any existing algebraic representative over the algebraic closure descends by lifting the Galois action through its universal property.

proof_stages.label: Bloch-Srinivas decomposition and Abel-Jacobi surjectivity
proof_stages.summary: Using Bloch-Srinivas decomposition of the diagonal, together with Murre and Voisin's formulation over C, the Chow-zero support hypothesis identifies Ab^2(X_C) with the image of the Abel-Jacobi map and in fact with the intermediate Jacobian J^3(X_C).

proof_stages.label: Algebraically closed base change and completion
proof_stages.summary: A base-change lemma for algebraic representatives, proved via torsion and isogeny arguments, identifies Ab^2(X_{ ar Q})_C with Ab^2(X_C), giving the needed dimension equality and hence the desired l-adic cohomological isomorphism after descent.

proof_stages.label: Quadric fibration extension
proof_stages.summary: The speaker situates the second result in the fibration picture: after easy point/curve-base and degree-one projective-bundle cases, the degree-two case is handled by a Mumford-Beauville Prym construction for quadric fibrations over suitable surfaces, yielding another phantom abelian variety result.

examples.name: Rational-curve fibrations over a surface with large CH_0

examples.engagement_reason: The speaker sets up rational-curve fibrations over a surface with Chow zero not supported on a curve and reasons that the main theorem does not apply, then uses this as the case to analyze further.
